title: Cephalaria pilosa
taxonomy: Cephalaria pilosa is a species in the family Valerianaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Cephalaria, which consists of about 10 species of flowering plants. Cephalaria pilosa is native to Europe and Western Asia, and its classification is further supported by its position within the order Dipsacales. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics, including its hairy leaves and small white flowers. Cephalaria pilosa is also sometimes referred to as a close relative to the genus Patrinia.

intro: Cephalaria pilosa, also known as hairy pincushion or hairy cephalaria, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The health benefits of Cephalaria pilosa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its potential in treating various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders.
